grounding_source_rows: paper failure mode criminal_vs_restraining (legacy_025); v1 finding that relationship context was ignored
grounding_note: The paper's hardest carried-forward failure: FETCH defaulted to Stalking Orders even after learning the harasser was the user's child's father. FAPA restraining orders require a family/household/intimate relationship; stalking orders cover everyone else. The relationship is a fact victims often omit because they lead with the conduct. Unlike v1's role swaps, the user's own position (person seeking protection) never changes — only the qualifying relationship does. Safety-aware wording throughout.

scenarios.opening_query: A person keeps pounding on my door late at night and shouting at me. I want it to stop.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my child's father. We lived together until last year, and I want him kept away from me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my child's father — we lived together until last year. I want him kept away from me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the relationship between the user and the person causing fear
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a man from my old gym I barely know — we've never had any relationship at all.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a guy from my old gym — I barely know him, no relationship ever.
scenarios.vetting_note: legacy_025 rebuilt exactly; the paper showed FETCH asked the right question and still ignored the answer.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone from my past won't stop showing up where I am. I'm scared and need a court order.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's a former coworker — we never dated or lived together, he just fixated on me after I changed jobs.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's a former coworker — never anything between us. He fixated on me after I changed jobs.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the person is a family/household member or unrelated
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's my ex-husband — divorced two years ago, and this started when I began dating again.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's my ex-husband — this all started when I began dating again.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Someone from my past' is deliberately relationship-ambiguous, as real intakes are.

scenarios.opening_query: My neighbor watches me, follows me to my car, and leaves notes on my windshield. It's terrifying.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's not just a neighbor — we dated for six months last year, and he moved into the building after I ended it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We actually dated for six months — he moved into my building after I ended it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a past intimate relationship exists with the person
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I've never exchanged more than a hello with him — he's a complete stranger who moved in last spring.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I've never spoken to him beyond hello — a stranger who moved in last spring.
scenarios.vetting_note: The intimate-relationship history is exactly what victims omit when leading with conduct.

scenarios.opening_query: I keep getting threatening messages from someone who knows way too much about my daily routine.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I finally confirmed it's my ex-wife using burner numbers — we divorced eight months ago.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I confirmed it's my ex-wife on burner numbers — we divorced eight months ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the sender is a former spouse or household member
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The police traced it to a customer I banned from my shop — I'd never met him outside work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Police traced it to a customer I banned from the shop — no other connection.
scenarios.vetting_note: Anonymous-conduct opening; identity discovery is the natural late disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: My roommate's behavior has turned frightening and I need legal protection.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're not just roommates — we've been in a relationship for two years and share the lease and bills.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're actually a couple — two years together, shared lease and bills.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the household relationship is intimate/domestic
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He answered my spare-room ad six months ago — we're strangers who share a kitchen.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He just answered my room ad six months ago — strangers sharing a kitchen.
scenarios.vetting_note: Roommate cases sit precisely on FAPA's household-member line; the relationship's nature is the fact. (Note: some cohabitants qualify under FAPA regardless — the intimate-partner fact makes qualification unambiguous.)

scenarios.opening_query: An older man in my family situation keeps threatening me and showing up at my work.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's my stepfather and I lived in his house until three months ago.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's my stepfather — I lived in his house until three months ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the person is a family or former household member
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's my mother's neighbor who inserted himself into our family — no actual relation, never lived together.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's just my mom's neighbor who inserted himself — no relation, never lived together.
scenarios.vetting_note: Family-adjacent phrasing tests whether the household-member question gets asked at all.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone has been following me home from campus and my friends say I need to do something official.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the guy I dated freshman year — we broke up in the spring and it started after that.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the guy I dated freshman year — it started right after we broke up.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I have no idea who he is — campus security has photos but nobody recognizes him.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nobody knows who he is — campus security has photos but no ID.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dating-relationship FAPA coverage vs stranger stalking; young-adult voice varies the register.

scenarios.opening_query: I was attacked outside my apartment by someone I recognized, and I'm scared it will happen again.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Crime Victims
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was arrested and charged, and the DA's office wants to interview me — I need to understand the criminal case I'm now part of, and whether I should have my own lawyer.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He was arrested and charged — the DA wants to interview me, and I need to understand the criminal case and whether I need my own lawyer.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal prosecution is underway and what role the user has in it
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Police couldn't find him and no charges were filed — I want a civil order keeping him away from me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They never found him and nothing was filed — I want a civil order keeping him away.
scenarios.vetting_note: Paper's criminal_vs_restraining boundary: prosecution status determines whether this is a protective-order matter or criminal-case support.

scenarios.opening_query: A man I got a protective order against keeps violating it and nothing happens to him.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Crime Victims
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was finally convicted for the violations and the assault — now I want to sue him civilly for my therapy costs, lost work, and moving expenses.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He was convicted for the violations and the assault — now I want to sue him for my therapy costs, lost work, and moving expenses.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user seeks order enforcement or civil damages as a crime victim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The order expires next month and I need it renewed and actually enforced.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The order expires next month — I need it renewed and enforced.
scenarios.vetting_note: Post-conviction civil damages is the Crime Victims label's core; remedy sought is the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: My sister's ex keeps coming to our house looking for her and it's frightening everyone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: The order would be for ME — he's threatened me personally at my door, and I've never had any relationship with him; my sister doesn't even live here anymore.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It'd be protection for me — he threatens me at my own door, and I've never had any relationship with him. My sister moved out long ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: who needs protection and what their relationship to the person is
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my sister who needs the order — he's her ex-partner and she's back living with us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's for my sister — he's her ex and she lives with us again.
scenarios.vetting_note: Protected-person identity determines the statute: sibling-of-ex has no FAPA relationship with the harasser.

scenarios.opening_query: My elderly mother is being intimidated by someone who comes by her house demanding money.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's her live-in caregiver's son — he's gotten her to 'lend' him $30,000 over the past year and pressured her to add him to her bank account.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's her caregiver's son — he's taken $30,000 in 'loans' and pressured her onto adding him to her bank account.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the intimidation involves financial exploitation of an elder by a trusted person
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's a stranger running a contractor scam who's now harassing her for 'cancellation fees' — no money has changed hands.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's a stranger with a contractor scam demanding 'cancellation fees' — she hasn't paid him anything.
scenarios.vetting_note: Trusted-person exploitation reroutes to elder abuse (with its protective-order variant); stranger harassment stays SPO.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone I had to fire keeps contacting me at home and my family is getting scared.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Labor & Employment > General - Employer
scenarios.hidden_fact: His lawyer just filed a wrongful termination suit and the contact is about that — my business partner says we need employment counsel for the company, which worries me more than the calls.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His lawyer filed a wrongful termination suit — the contact is about that case, and the company needs employment counsel.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the contact stems from pending employment litigation against the business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no lawsuit — he shows up at my house at night and follows my wife's car; the police suggested a stalking order.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No lawsuit — he shows up at night and follows my wife's car. Police suggested a stalking order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Personal-safety vs company-litigation reading of the same contact; the filed-suit fact routes it.
